resources.name: typematerial
resources.description: Type material designated to names. Type material should only be associated with the original name, not with a recombination.

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Optional unique identifier for the specimen. If possible use the existing specimen identifier, e.g. the collection/institution code and catalogue number. If coming from a Darwin Core world dwc:occurrenceID is a great fit.

fields.name: nameID
fields.title: nameID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of name IDs pointing to the typified name of this specimen. Each ID must refer to an existing Name.ID within this data package. See best practices for details on how to concatenate multi values.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: citation
fields.title: citation
fields.description: Material citation of the type material, i.e. type specimen. The citation is ideally given in the verbatim form as it was used in the original publication of the name or the subsequent designation. Otherwise it is recommended to follow the material citation guidelines published by European Journal of Taxonomy. If atomized fields below are given a citation is not needed. Otherwise it is required.

fields.name: status
fields.title: status
fields.description: In case multiple names have been linked to the specimen through concatenated values in nameID, a list of comma concatenated status values can be given in the same order as the name IDs. If a single value is given it will be used for all names.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A referenceID pointing to the Reference table indicating the publication of the type designation. Most often this is equivalent to the original names referenceID, but for subsequent designations a later reference should be cited.

fields.name: page
fields.title: page
fields.description: The exact single page number where the type designation was published in the linked reference. If the value spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: country
fields.title: country
fields.description: The country of the type locality. Preferably as ISO codes.

fields.name: locality
fields.title: locality
fields.description: The type locality. Ideally from largest area to smallest.

fields.name: latitude
fields.title: latitude
fields.description: Decimal latitude of the type locality given in WGS84

fields.name: longitude
fields.title: longitude
fields.description: Decimal longitude of the type locality given in WGS84

fields.name: altitude
fields.title: altitude
fields.description: Altitude of the type locality. Ideally given as meters above mean seal level. Depth should be given as negative altitudes.

fields.name: host
fields.title: host
fields.description: Indicates the host organism from which the type specimen was obtained (symbiotype).

fields.name: date
fields.title: date
fields.description: Date the type material was gathered. Recommended to be given as ISO 8601 dates.

fields.name: collector
fields.title: collector
fields.description: The collectors name

fields.name: institutionCode
fields.title: institutionCode
fields.description: The name or acronym in use by the institution having custody of the material.

fields.name: catalogNumber
fields.title: catalogNumber
fields.description: The identifier for the specimen in a collection.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A link to further information about the specimen, e.g. as provided by the institute holding the collection.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Any further remarks on the type material.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: reference
resources.description: Structured bibliographic references with a unique id to refer to from other entities. References can be given either as a simple, single citation string, or in a structured form. ColDP supports fully parsed references in CSV based on the CSL-JSON format. Alternatively references can also be provided in the native file formats for the well established BibTex or CSL-JSON formats. See the sections below with for how to share alternative formats that do not conform to tabular CSV/TSV files.

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: The local identifier for the reference as used in referenceID in other entities.

fields.name: alternativeID
fields.title: alternativeID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of alternative identifiers for the reference. Every alternative identifier must be a URI/URN/URL or given in the form of scope:id. See identifiers for all details and common scopes.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: citation
fields.title: citation
fields.description: Full bibliographic citation as one single string as an alternative to the rest of the more structured fields. If individual fields are given the full citation can be ignored.

fields.name: author
fields.title: author
fields.description: The author(s) of the work. If multiple authors use a style that can safely be parsed. Recommended are 2 common forms:

fields.name: authorID
fields.title: authorID
fields.description: List of Author.ID identifiers separated by a comma that act as authors for this reference. Authors must exist in the local data package.

fields.name: editor
fields.title: editor
fields.description: The editor(s) of the work. See author for recommendations how to supply person names.

fields.name: editorID
fields.title: editorID
fields.description: List of Author.ID identifiers separated by a comma that act as editors for this reference. Authors must exist in the local data package.

fields.name: title
fields.title: title
fields.description: The title of the work. In case of journal articles the article title, not the journal itself.

fields.name: titleShort
fields.title: titleShort
fields.description: The abbreviated title of the work.

fields.name: containerAuthor
fields.title: containerAuthor
fields.description: Author(s) of the container holding the item, e.g. the book author for a book chapter. See author for recommendations how to supply person names.

fields.name: containerAuthorID
fields.title: containerAuthorID
fields.description: List of Author.ID identifiers separated by a comma that act as the container authors for this reference. Authors must exist in the local data package.

fields.name: containerTitle
fields.title: containerTitle
fields.description: Title of the container holding the item, e.g. the book title for a book chapter, the journal title for a journal article. The containerTitle should exclude volume, edition, pages and other specifics.

fields.name: containerTitleShort
fields.title: containerTitleShort
fields.description: The abbreviated container title.

fields.name: collectionTitle
fields.title: collectionTitle
fields.description: Title of the collection holding the item, e.g. the series title for a book.

fields.name: collectionEditor
fields.title: collectionEditor
fields.description: Editor(s) of the collection holding the item, e.g. the series editor for a book.

fields.name: collectionEditorID
fields.title: collectionEditorID
fields.description: List of Author.ID identifiers separated by a comma that act as collection editors for this reference. Authors must exist in the local data package.

fields.name: volume
fields.title: volume
fields.description: (container) volume number holding the item, e.g. 2 when citing a chapter from book volume 2.

fields.name: issue
fields.title: issue
fields.description: (container) issue holding the item, e.g. 5 when citing a journal article from journal volume 2, issue 5.

fields.name: edition
fields.title: edition
fields.description: (container) edition holding the item, e.g. 3 when citing a chapter in the third edition of a book.

fields.name: page
fields.title: page
fields.description: Range of pages the item (e.g. a journal article) covers in a container (e.g. a journal issue)

fields.name: publisher
fields.title: publisher
fields.description: Name of the publisher

fields.name: publisherPlace
fields.title: publisherPlace
fields.description: Geographic location of the publisher

fields.name: version
fields.title: version
fields.description: Version of the item or dataset

fields.name: isbn
fields.title: isbn
fields.description: International Standard Book Number

fields.name: issn
fields.title: issn
fields.description: International Standard Serial Number

fields.name: doi
fields.title: doi
fields.description: The DOI of the reference

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A URL link to the reference. A link to a webpage for electronic resources. url in CSL-JSON terminology, but we prefer link to be consistent with other ColDP entities.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Additional comments about the reference. note in CSL-JSON terminology, but we prefer remarks to be consistent with other ColDP entities.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: taxon
resources.description: An accepted name with a taxonomic classification given either as a parent-child relation or as a flat, denormalized record.

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Unique taxon identifier that is referred to elsewhere via taxonID.

fields.name: alternativeID
fields.title: alternativeID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of alternative identifiers for the taxonomic concept. Every alternative identifier must be a URI/URN/URL or given in the form of scope:id. See identifiers for all details and common scopes.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: parentID
fields.title: parentID
fields.description: The direct parent taxon’s ID in the classification. This is the preferred way of exchanging a hierarchy and takes precedence over any classification given in the denormalized fields.

fields.name: nameID
fields.title: nameID
fields.description: Pointer to the accepted name referring to an existing Name.ID within this data package.

fields.name: namePhrase
fields.title: namePhrase
fields.description: An optional, unrestricted, lose phrase appended to the name just for this taxon. E.g. the phrase “sensu lato” may be added to the name to describe this taxon more precisely.

fields.name: accordingToID
fields.title: accordingToID
fields.description: A reference ID to the publication that established the taxonomic concept used by this taxon. The author & year of the reference will be used to qualify the name with sensu AUTHOR, YEAR. The ID must refer to an existing Reference.ID within this data package.

fields.name: accordingToPage
fields.title: accordingToPage
fields.description: The exact single page number where the taxonomic concept was treated. If the treatment spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: accordingToPageLink
fields.title: accordingToPageLink
fields.description: A URL to the exact page where the taxonomic concept was published. If the treatment spans multiple pages, the link to the first page should be given.

fields.name: provisional
fields.title: provisional
fields.description: A flag indicating that the taxon is only provisionally accepted and should be handled with care.

fields.name: scrutinizer
fields.title: scrutinizer
fields.description: Name of the person who is the latest scrutinizer who revised or reviewed the taxonomic concept.

fields.name: scrutinizerID
fields.title: scrutinizerID
fields.description: Identifier for the scrutinizer. Highly recommended are ORCID ids.

fields.name: extinct
fields.title: extinct
fields.description: Nullable flag indicating that the taxon is extinct (true) or extant (false). This includes species that died out recently.

fields.name: temporalRangeStart
fields.title: temporalRangeStart
fields.description: Earliest appearance of the taxon in the geological time scale. Recommended values are geochronological names from the official International Commission on Stratigraphy (ICS) or million years before present, given with the unit Ma after the number, e.g. 17.4 Ma.

fields.name: temporalRangeEnd
fields.title: temporalRangeEnd
fields.description: Latest appearance of the taxon in the geological time scale. Recommended values are geochronological names from the official International Commission on Stratigraphy (ICS) or million years before present, given with the unit Ma after the number, e.g. 17.4 Ma.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of reference IDs supporting the taxonomic concept that has been reviewed by the scrutinizer. Each ID must refer to an existing Reference.ID within this data package. See best practices for details on how to concatenate multi values.

fields.name: species
fields.title: species
fields.description: The species binomial the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: section
fields.title: section
fields.description: The (botanical) section the taxon is classified in. Considered a botanical rank below subgenus, not a zoological above family. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subgenus
fields.title: subgenus
fields.description: The subgenus the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: genus
fields.title: genus
fields.description: The genus the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subtribe
fields.title: subtribe
fields.description: The subtribe the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: tribe
fields.title: tribe
fields.description: The tribe the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subfamily
fields.title: subfamily
fields.description: The subfamily the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: family
fields.title: family
fields.description: The family the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: superfamily
fields.title: superfamily
fields.description: The superfamily the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: suborder
fields.title: suborder
fields.description: The suborder the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: order
fields.title: order
fields.description: The order the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subclass
fields.title: subclass
fields.description: The subclass the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: class
fields.title: class
fields.description: The class the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subphylum
fields.title: subphylum
fields.description: The subphylum the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: phylum
fields.title: phylum
fields.description: The phylum the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: kingdom
fields.title: kingdom
fields.description: The kingdom the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: ordinal
fields.title: ordinal
fields.description: A integer to specify an optional custom sort order for sibling taxa sharing the same parentID in the datasets. This can be used to define a traditional ordering of orders and families for example and can be existing for parts of the dataset, e.g. higher ranks, only. The natural ordering of integers from small to large should be applied. Not that this does not have to be a unique, global index.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A link to a webpage provided by the source depicting the taxon.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Any further taxonomic remarks.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: vernacularname
resources.description: A vernacular or common name for a taxon.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: Pointer to the taxon referring to an existing Taxon.ID within this data package.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: name
fields.title: name
fields.description: The vernacular name in the original script.

fields.name: transliteration
fields.title: transliteration
fields.description: An optional transliteration of the vernacular name into the latin script.

fields.name: language
fields.title: language
fields.description: Language of the vernacular name given as an ISO 639-3 letter code.

fields.name: country
fields.title: country
fields.description: Country this vernacular name is used in given as an ISO 3166-2 letter code.

fields.name: area
fields.title: area
fields.description: Optional area describing the geographic use of the vernacular name in free text within the given country.

fields.name: sex
fields.title: sex
fields.description: Optional sex of the organism this vernacular name is restricted to.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: Pointer to the reference that supports this vernacular name. Refers to an existing Reference.ID within this data package.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the vernacular name.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: treatment
resources.description:
Treatment documents are stored as individual files in the treatments/ subdirectory, one file per taxon.
Files are named using the taxonID of the associated taxon with a format-specific extension:
.html (HTML), .txt (plain text or Markdown), .xml (TaxPub or TaxonX), or .pdf (PDF).
There is no tabular treatment file; the directory itself is the resource.

resources.name: namerelation
resources.description: A directed nomenclatural name relation. See #name-relations for examples and definitions.

fields.name: nameID
fields.title: nameID
fields.description: The subject name this relation originates from. Refers to an existing Name.ID or NameUsage.ID within this data package.

fields.name: relatedNameID
fields.title: relatedNameID
fields.description: The object name this relation relates to. Refers to an existing Name.ID or NameUsage.ID within this data package.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: type
fields.title: type
fields.description: The kind of directed nomenclatural relation.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: The reference or nomenclatural act where this nomenclatural relation was established.

fields.name: page
fields.title: page
fields.description: The exact single page number where the nomenclatural relation was published in the linked reference. If the value spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the relation.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: speciesestimate
resources.description: An estimation of the number of species for a given higher taxon, e.g. a family. The estimation must be based on a reference and should give the number of species according to a certain “type” that is expected to exist.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: The higher taxon’s ID that is the estimate refers to.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A mandatory reference ID that supports the estimate and also provides a temporal context.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the species estimate. Often used to explain the method used when the estimate is not directly taken from a publication.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: name
resources.description: Names can be shared in a structured way using various fields, but rank, scientificName and authorship alone are sufficient. See for examples and rationales. A structured scientificName can be given using the following fields:

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Unique name identifier that is referred to elsewhere via nameID.

fields.name: alternativeID
fields.title: alternativeID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of alternative identifiers for the name. Every alternative identifier must be a URI/URN/URL or given in the form of scope:id. See identifiers for all details and common scopes.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: basionymID
fields.title: basionymID
fields.description: Identifier of the name which is the original combination of this name. Also known as the basionym. Contrary to the strict basionym definition it is acceptable to populate this field also for original names which should point to itself. A basionym is a terminal relationship which cannot be “chained”. The original name itself should not have another basionym relation to another name. When the basionym was established as a nomen novum to replace another name, e.g. a homonym, it should not use basionymID to refer to the replaced name (which has an entirely different epithet), but use the NameRelation with type=replacement name instead. Note there is an alternative way to share the information about an original name by using a NameRelation with type=basionym. The field basionymID exists for simplicity and because it is an important information to be shared.

fields.name: scientificName
fields.title: scientificName
fields.description: Required scientific name excluding the authorship

fields.name: authorship
fields.title: authorship
fields.description: Authorship of the scientificName

fields.name: rank
fields.title: rank
fields.description: The rank of the name preferably given in case insensitive english. The recommended vocabulary is included in rank_enum.

fields.name: uninomial
fields.title: uninomial
fields.description: The single-word name of generic or higher rank names.

fields.name: genus
fields.title: genus
fields.description: The genus part of a bi/trinomial. Note that for generic names the uninomial field should be used, not genus!

fields.name: infragenericEpithet
fields.title: infragenericEpithet
fields.description: The infrageneric epithet. Used as the terminal epithet for names at infrageneric ranks and optionally also for bi/trinomials In zoological names often the subgenus.

fields.name: specificEpithet
fields.title: specificEpithet
fields.description: The specific epithet in case of bi/trinomials.

fields.name: infraspecificEpithet
fields.title: infraspecificEpithet
fields.description: The infraspecific epithet in case of bi/trinomials.

fields.name: cultivarEpithet
fields.title: cultivarEpithet
fields.description: The name of the cultivar for name governed by the cultivar code.

fields.name: notho
fields.title: notho
fields.description: For named hybrids the part of the name which is considered a hybrid and which usually is prefixed with the hybrid marker ×. One of:

fields.name: originalSpelling
fields.title: originalSpelling
fields.description: Flag indicating that the name is given in it’s original spelling when an emendation exists. Only use the flag if there is a known correction existing. The originalSpelling is usually indicated by placing [sic] after the name. A originalSpelling=false flag instead is indicating that the name is a corrected spelling, usually indicated by placing corrig. after the name. In most cases when it is unknown or the original spelling was never revised leave this flag empty.

fields.name: combinationAuthorship
fields.title: combinationAuthorship
fields.description: The authorteam of the main authorship for the exact combination (not the original combination). Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: combinationAuthorshipID
fields.title: combinationAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for authors of the exact combination (not the original combination). Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If combinationAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: combinationExAuthorship
fields.title: combinationExAuthorship
fields.description: The ex-authors part of the main authorship for the very combination (not the original combination). The ex prefix as normally found in the authorship should not be included here. Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: combinationExAuthorshipID
fields.title: combinationExAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for ex-authors of the exact combination (not the original combination). Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If combinationExAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: combinationAuthorshipYear
fields.title: combinationAuthorshipYear
fields.description: The year given in the authorship for the very combination (not the original combination), given without brackets.

fields.name: basionymAuthorship
fields.title: basionymAuthorship
fields.description: The authorteam of the original name normally found in brackets, but given here without brackets. Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: basionymAuthorshipID
fields.title: basionymAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for authors of the original combination (basionym) normally found in brackets. Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If basionymAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: basionymExAuthorship
fields.title: basionymExAuthorship
fields.description: The ex-authors of the original name normally found in brackets, but given here without brackets. The ex prefix as normally found in the authorship should not be included here. Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: basionymExAuthorshipID
fields.title: basionymExAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for ex-authors of the original combination (basionym) normally found in brackets. Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If basionymExAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: basionymAuthorshipYear
fields.title: basionymAuthorshipYear
fields.description: The year given in the authorship for the original combination normally found in brackets, but given here without brackets.

fields.name: code
fields.title: code
fields.description: The nomenclatural code the name falls under.

fields.name: status
fields.title: status
fields.description: The broad nomenclatural status of the name. For the exact status note, e.g. nomen nudum, the remarks field should additionally be used Alternatively a URI or simple name from a class of the NOMEN ontology can be used.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A pointer to a Reference that is the publication in which the scientificName was originally established under the rules of the associated nomenclatural code.

fields.name: publishedInYear
fields.title: publishedInYear
fields.description: The effective year the name was published, given as a 4 digit integer. It is the year that is nomenclaturally relevant for the given combination. In most cases this will be the same as the publication year given in the linked reference record via referenceID. But in some cases this might be different.

fields.name: publishedInPage
fields.title: publishedInPage
fields.description: The exact single page number where the name was published. If the description spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: publishedInPageLink
fields.title: publishedInPageLink
fields.description: A URL to the exact page where the name was published. If the description spans multiple pages, the link to the first page should be given.

fields.name: gender
fields.title: gender
fields.description: Gender of the name, i.e. the genus in case of bi/trinomials. Values for the gender field should be one of masculine, feminine or neuter.

fields.name: genderAgreement
fields.title: genderAgreement
fields.description: Flag that indicates for bi/trinomials whether the (infra)species epithet must follow and agree with the gender of the genus.

fields.name: etymology
fields.title: etymology
fields.description: Etymology of the name, i.e. the origin or meaning of the words forming the scientific name. Should be a short human readable paragraph.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A link to a webpage provided by the source depicting the name.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Additional nomenclatural remarks about the name. Often indicating its status or relevant rules in the code.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: author
resources.description: Normalised and structured authors that can be referred to by names, references and taxon scrutinizers. All entities also allow to specify a modifedBy field which must reference an Author identifier here.

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Unique identifier for the author / person. Can be referenced from any modifiedBy field.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: alternativeID
fields.title: alternativeID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of alternative identifiers for the author. Every alternative identifier must be in the form of scope:id. See identifiers for all details and common scopes. Recommended identifier scopes for authors are orcid, ipni, wikidata & viaf.

fields.name: given
fields.title: given
fields.description: List of given names, concatenated by a comma.

fields.name: family
fields.title: family
fields.description: The family name including any leading particles if existing.

fields.name: suffix
fields.title: suffix
fields.description: Optional suffix to distinguish persons with identical surnames. In well known cases of father and son, the son should be distinguished by ‘f.’ or ‘filius’ in the suffix.

fields.name: abbreviationBotany
fields.title: abbreviationBotany
fields.description: Standard form (official abbreviation) of the persons name for use in a botanical author citation.

fields.name: alternativeNames
fields.title: alternativeNames
fields.description: A | separated list of alternative names this person is known under.

fields.name: sex
fields.title: sex
fields.description: Biological sex of the person.

fields.name: country
fields.title: country
fields.description: Country of citizenship. Preferably as ISO code. If multiple concatenated by a comma.

fields.name: birth
fields.title: birth
fields.description: Date of birth, given as an ISO date string.

fields.name: birthPlace
fields.title: birthPlace
fields.description: Location the person was born at.

fields.name: death
fields.title: death
fields.description: Date of death, given as an ISO date string.

fields.name: affiliation
fields.title: affiliation
fields.description: Institution(s) the author is affiliated with.

fields.name: interest
fields.title: interest
fields.description: List of taxonomic groups the person has worked on.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: List of sources where the information was taken from or further information can be found about the author.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A link to a webpage provided by the source depicting the author.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the person.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: media
resources.description: Multimedia items for a taxon such as an image, audio or video.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: Pointer to the taxon referring to an existing Taxon.ID within this data package.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: url
fields.title: url
fields.description: The URL that resolves to the media item itself, not a webpage that depicts it.

fields.name: type
fields.title: type
fields.description: The MIME-type of the media item the url identifies. Preferably the full type/subtype combination, e.g image/jpeg, but the primary type alone is sufficient (image, video, audio).

fields.name: title
fields.title: title
fields.description: Optional title for the item.

fields.name: creator
fields.title: creator
fields.description: Author of the media item.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: Optional webpage from the source this media item is shown on.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the media item.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: distribution
resources.description: A structured distribution record for a taxon in a given area.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: Pointer to the taxon referring to an existing Taxon.ID within this data package.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: areaID
fields.title: areaID
fields.description: The identifier/code for the geographic area this distribution record is about. The value must be taken from the gazetteer this record declares. E.g. ISO 2 letter country codes, TDWG codes or TEOW identifiers. If the TEXT gazetteer is used only the free text area should be given with no areaID.

fields.name: area
fields.title: area
fields.description: The geographic area this distribution record is about. The value provides a human label for the area specified by areaID. Free text values can be provided here when the gazetteer is set to TEXT.

fields.name: gazetteer
fields.title: gazetteer
fields.description: The geographic gazetteer the area is defined in. If none is given defaults to free TEXT.

fields.name: pathway
fields.title: pathway
fields.description: The process by which the taxon came to be in the given area at the given time.

fields.name: lifeStage
fields.title: lifeStage
fields.description: Optional restriction of the distribution record to just a particular life stage of the species.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: Pointer to the reference that supports this distribution. Refers to an existing Reference.ID within this data package.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the distribution.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: taxonproperty
resources.description: A flexible, generic way to assign arbitrary property values to a taxon. It can be used to share species profiles, traits, descriptions and any other dynamic information about a taxon. Every property value can optionally be referenced and ordered.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: The subject taxon the property is about.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: property
fields.title: property
fields.description: The required name of the property the value is assigned to. For example a text label like “Biology” or “Illustration”, a Plinian core term or some Wikidata P value like P2974.

fields.name: value
fields.title: value
fields.description: A required free text value for the given property. If markup is needed Markdown is preferred.

fields.name: ordinal
fields.title: ordinal
fields.description: An integer to specify an optional custom sort order for property values sharing the same taxonID in the dataset.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: An optional reference where this property value was documented or who asserted it.

fields.name: page
fields.title: page
fields.description: The exact single page number where the property value was published in the linked reference. If the value spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the property value.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: synonym
resources.description: A synonymous name for a taxon. Note that the same name can be linked to multiple taxa by having several Synonym records to model pro parte synonyms.

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Optional unique identifier for the synonym. If given it should not clash with the taxon ids.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: Pointer to the taxon that this synonym is used for. For pro parte synonyms with multiple accepted names several synonym records sharing the same name but having different taxonIDs should be created. Refers to an existing Taxon.ID within this data package.

fields.name: nameID
fields.title: nameID
fields.description: Pointer to the synonymous name referring to an existing Name.ID within this data package.

fields.name: namePhrase
fields.title: namePhrase
fields.description: An optional, unrestricted, lose phrase appended to the name just for this synonym. E.g. the phrase “sensu lato” may be added to the name to describe this synonym more precisely. Or “auct. mult.” or “auct. amer.” for misapplied names that cannot refer to a single publication. Misapplied names that refer to a single publication should use accordingToID instead.

fields.name: accordingToID
fields.title: accordingToID
fields.description: A reference ID to the publication that established the taxonomic concept used by this taxon. The author & year of the reference will be used to qualify the name with sensu AUTHOR, YEAR. Strongly recommended in case of misapplied names. The ID must refer to an existing Reference.ID within this data package.

fields.name: accordingToPage
fields.title: accordingToPage
fields.description: The exact single page number where the synonym was treated. If the treatment spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: accordingToPageLink
fields.title: accordingToPageLink
fields.description: A URL to the exact page where the synonym was published. If the treatment spans multiple pages, the link to the first page should be given.

fields.name: status
fields.title: status
fields.description: The kind of synonym. One of synonym, ambiguous synonym or misapplied. Defaults to synonym.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of reference IDs supporting the synonym status of the name. Each ID must refer to an existing Reference.ID within this data package.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A link to a webpage provided by the source depicting the synonym.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Any further taxonomic remarks.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: taxonconceptrelation
resources.description: A directed taxon relation representing RCC5 taxon concept assertions.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: The subject taxon this relation originates from.

fields.name: relatedTaxonID
fields.title: relatedTaxonID
fields.description: The object this taxon relates to.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A reference where this relation was documented or who asserted it.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the concept relation.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: speciesinteraction
resources.description: A directed taxon relation representing species interactions. Different to a TaxonConceptRelation a species interaction can also point to a species (name) outside of the local dataset.

fields.name: taxonID
fields.title: taxonID
fields.description: The subject taxon the species interaction is about. Always required to point to an existing taxonID in the local dataset.

fields.name: relatedTaxonID
fields.title: relatedTaxonID
fields.description: The related taxon this interaction is describing. If given it must refer to a local taxonID from the dataset. If missing, the ‘relatedTaxonScientificName’ must be given instead.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: relatedTaxonScientificName
fields.title: relatedTaxonScientificName
fields.description: The scientificName of the related taxon this interaction is describing. Includes the authorship if known. It is mutually exclusive with relatedTaxonID and if given no relatedTaxonID should exist. The relatedTaxonScientificName can be used to document species interactions without the need to have full blown name and taxon records.

fields.name: type
fields.title: type
fields.description: The kind of directed species interaction. Each interaction exists also in reverse to allow the alternative relatedTaxonScientificName field to be used. Species interaction types are heavily inspired by https://www.globalbioticinteractions.org and the OBO Relation Ontology http://www.ontobee.org/ontology/RO to which all entries are mapped.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A reference where the interaction was documented.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Remarks about the species interaction.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.

resources.name: nameusage
resources.description: As a simpler alternative to the 3 entities Name, Taxon and Synonym a single NameUsage entity can be supplied. A NameUsage record can either be an accepted Taxon or a Synonym and is easily distinguished by its status. A NameUsage.ID acts both as a taxonID and nameID if referred to from other table, e.g TypeMaterial or VernacularName. For synonyms the parentID field is used to link to the accepted taxon. All properties available in the individual entities can also be used for the single NameUsage:  There are two clashing properties that exist both on a Name and Taxon/Synonym, but which have a slightly different meaning. Therefore the following properties deviate slightly from their usage in their classic version:

fields.name: ID
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Unique name identifier that is referred to elsewhere via nameID.

fields.name: alternativeID
fields.title: alternativeID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of alternative identifiers for the name. Every alternative identifier must be a URI/URN/URL or given in the form of scope:id. See identifiers for all details and common scopes.

fields.name: nameAlternativeID
fields.title: nameAlternativeID
fields.description: A comma concatenated list of alternative identifiers for the name. Every alternative identifier must be a URI/URN/URL or given in the form of scope:id. See identifiers for all details and common scopes.

fields.name: sourceID
fields.title: sourceID
fields.description: Optional identifier for the source this record came from as listed in the metadata.yaml

fields.name: parentID
fields.title: parentID
fields.description: The direct parent taxon’s ID in the classification. This is the preferred way of exchanging a hierarchy and takes precedence over any classification given in the denormalized fields.

fields.name: basionymID
fields.title: basionymID
fields.description: Identifier of the name which is the original combination of this name. Also known as the basionym. Contrary to the strict basionym definition it is acceptable to populate this field also for original names which should point to itself. A basionym is a terminal relationship which cannot be “chained”. The original name itself should not have another basionym relation to another name. When the basionym was established as a nomen novum to replace another name, e.g. a homonym, it should not use basionymID to refer to the replaced name (which has an entirely different epithet), but use the NameRelation with type=replacement name instead. Note there is an alternative way to share the information about an original name by using a NameRelation with type=basionym. The field basionymID exists for simplicity and because it is an important information to be shared.

fields.name: status
fields.title: status
fields.description: The broad nomenclatural status of the name. For the exact status note, e.g. nomen nudum, the remarks field should additionally be used Alternatively a URI or simple name from a class of the NOMEN ontology can be used.

fields.name: scientificName
fields.title: scientificName
fields.description: Required scientific name excluding the authorship

fields.name: authorship
fields.title: authorship
fields.description: Authorship of the scientificName

fields.name: rank
fields.title: rank
fields.description: The rank of the name preferably given in case insensitive english. The recommended vocabulary is included in rank_enum.

fields.name: notho
fields.title: notho
fields.description: For named hybrids the part of the name which is considered a hybrid and which usually is prefixed with the hybrid marker ×. One of:

fields.name: originalSpelling
fields.title: originalSpelling
fields.description: Flag indicating that the name is given in it’s original spelling when an emendation exists. Only use the flag if there is a known correction existing. The originalSpelling is usually indicated by placing [sic] after the name. A originalSpelling=false flag instead is indicating that the name is a corrected spelling, usually indicated by placing corrig. after the name. In most cases when it is unknown or the original spelling was never revised leave this flag empty.

fields.name: uninomial
fields.title: uninomial
fields.description: The single-word name of generic or higher rank names.

fields.name: genericName
fields.title: genericName
fields.description: The genus part of a bi/trinomial. Note that for generic names the uninomial field should be used, not genus!

fields.name: infragenericEpithet
fields.title: infragenericEpithet
fields.description: The infrageneric epithet. Used as the terminal epithet for names at infrageneric ranks and optionally also for bi/trinomials In zoological names often the subgenus.

fields.name: specificEpithet
fields.title: specificEpithet
fields.description: The specific epithet in case of bi/trinomials.

fields.name: infraspecificEpithet
fields.title: infraspecificEpithet
fields.description: The infraspecific epithet in case of bi/trinomials.

fields.name: cultivarEpithet
fields.title: cultivarEpithet
fields.description: The name of the cultivar for name governed by the cultivar code.

fields.name: combinationAuthorship
fields.title: combinationAuthorship
fields.description: The authorteam of the main authorship for the exact combination (not the original combination). Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: combinationAuthorshipID
fields.title: combinationAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for authors of the exact combination (not the original combination). Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If combinationAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: combinationExAuthorship
fields.title: combinationExAuthorship
fields.description: The ex-authors part of the main authorship for the very combination (not the original combination). The ex prefix as normally found in the authorship should not be included here. Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: combinationExAuthorshipID
fields.title: combinationExAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for ex-authors of the exact combination (not the original combination). Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If combinationExAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: combinationAuthorshipYear
fields.title: combinationAuthorshipYear
fields.description: The year given in the authorship for the very combination (not the original combination), given without brackets.

fields.name: basionymAuthorship
fields.title: basionymAuthorship
fields.description: The authorteam of the original name normally found in brackets, but given here without brackets. Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: basionymAuthorshipID
fields.title: basionymAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for authors of the original combination (basionym) normally found in brackets. Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If basionymAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: basionymExAuthorship
fields.title: basionymExAuthorship
fields.description: The ex-authors of the original name normally found in brackets, but given here without brackets. The ex prefix as normally found in the authorship should not be included here. Multiple authors should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol.

fields.name: basionymExAuthorshipID
fields.title: basionymExAuthorshipID
fields.description: A list of identifiers for ex-authors of the original combination (basionym) normally found in brackets. Multiple author identifiers should be concatenated with a pipe | symbol. If basionymExAuthorship is given, the order and number of author names and identifiers must always match up. Author identifiers must refer to an existing Author.ID within this data package.

fields.name: basionymAuthorshipYear
fields.title: basionymAuthorshipYear
fields.description: The year given in the authorship for the original combination normally found in brackets, but given here without brackets.

fields.name: namePhrase
fields.title: namePhrase
fields.description: An optional, unrestricted, lose phrase appended to the name just for this taxon. E.g. the phrase “sensu lato” may be added to the name to describe this taxon more precisely.

fields.name: nameReferenceID
fields.title: nameReferenceID
fields.description: A pointer to a Reference that is the publication in which the scientificName was originally established under the rules of the associated nomenclatural code.

fields.name: namePublishedInYear
fields.title: namePublishedInYear
fields.description: The effective year the name was published, given as a 4 digit integer. It is the year that is nomenclaturally relevant for the given combination. In most cases this will be the same as the publication year given in the linked reference record via referenceID. But in some cases this might be different.

fields.name: namePublishedInPage
fields.title: namePublishedInPage
fields.description: The exact single page number where the name was published. If the description spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: namePublishedInPageLink
fields.title: namePublishedInPageLink
fields.description: A URL to the exact page where the name was published. If the description spans multiple pages, the link to the first page should be given.

fields.name: gender
fields.title: gender
fields.description: Gender of the name, i.e. the genus in case of bi/trinomials. Values for the gender field should be one of masculine, feminine or neuter.

fields.name: genderAgreement
fields.title: genderAgreement
fields.description: Flag that indicates for bi/trinomials whether the (infra)species epithet must follow and agree with the gender of the genus.

fields.name: etymology
fields.title: etymology
fields.description: Etymology of the name, i.e. the origin or meaning of the words forming the scientific name. Should be a short human readable paragraph.

fields.name: code
fields.title: code
fields.description: The nomenclatural code the name falls under.

fields.name: nameStatus
fields.title: nameStatus
fields.description: The broad nomenclatural status of the name. For the exact status note, e.g. nomen nudum, the remarks field should additionally be used Alternatively a URI or simple name from a class of the NOMEN ontology can be used.

fields.name: accordingToID
fields.title: accordingToID
fields.description: A reference ID to the publication that established the taxonomic concept used by this taxon. The author & year of the reference will be used to qualify the name with sensu AUTHOR, YEAR. The ID must refer to an existing Reference.ID within this data package.

fields.name: accordingToPage
fields.title: accordingToPage
fields.description: The exact single page number where the taxonomic concept was treated. If the treatment spans multiple pages, the first page should be given.

fields.name: accordingToPageLink
fields.title: accordingToPageLink
fields.description: A URL to the exact page where the taxonomic concept was published. If the treatment spans multiple pages, the link to the first page should be given.

fields.name: referenceID
fields.title: referenceID
fields.description: A pointer to a Reference that is the publication in which the scientificName was originally established under the rules of the associated nomenclatural code.

fields.name: scrutinizer
fields.title: scrutinizer
fields.description: Name of the person who is the latest scrutinizer who revised or reviewed the taxonomic concept.

fields.name: scrutinizerID
fields.title: scrutinizerID
fields.description: Identifier for the scrutinizer. Highly recommended are ORCID ids.

fields.name: extinct
fields.title: extinct
fields.description: Nullable flag indicating that the taxon is extinct (true) or extant (false). This includes species that died out recently.

fields.name: temporalRangeStart
fields.title: temporalRangeStart
fields.description: Earliest appearance of the taxon in the geological time scale. Recommended values are geochronological names from the official International Commission on Stratigraphy (ICS) or million years before present, given with the unit Ma after the number, e.g. 17.4 Ma.

fields.name: temporalRangeEnd
fields.title: temporalRangeEnd
fields.description: Latest appearance of the taxon in the geological time scale. Recommended values are geochronological names from the official International Commission on Stratigraphy (ICS) or million years before present, given with the unit Ma after the number, e.g. 17.4 Ma.

fields.name: species
fields.title: species
fields.description: The species binomial the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: section
fields.title: section
fields.description: The (botanical) section the taxon is classified in. Considered a botanical rank below subgenus, not a zoological above family. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subgenus
fields.title: subgenus
fields.description: The subgenus the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: genus
fields.title: genus
fields.description: The genus part of a bi/trinomial. Note that for generic names the uninomial field should be used, not genus!

fields.name: subtribe
fields.title: subtribe
fields.description: The subtribe the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: tribe
fields.title: tribe
fields.description: The tribe the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subfamily
fields.title: subfamily
fields.description: The subfamily the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: family
fields.title: family
fields.description: The family the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: superfamily
fields.title: superfamily
fields.description: The superfamily the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: suborder
fields.title: suborder
fields.description: The suborder the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: order
fields.title: order
fields.description: The order the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subclass
fields.title: subclass
fields.description: The subclass the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: class
fields.title: class
fields.description: The class the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: subphylum
fields.title: subphylum
fields.description: The subphylum the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: phylum
fields.title: phylum
fields.description: The phylum the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: kingdom
fields.title: kingdom
fields.description: The kingdom the taxon is classified in. If parentID is given this field is ignored.

fields.name: ordinal
fields.title: ordinal
fields.description: A integer to specify an optional custom sort order for sibling taxa sharing the same parentID in the datasets. This can be used to define a traditional ordering of orders and families for example and can be existing for parts of the dataset, e.g. higher ranks, only. The natural ordering of integers from small to large should be applied. Not that this does not have to be a unique, global index.

fields.name: link
fields.title: link
fields.description: A link to a webpage provided by the source depicting the name.

fields.name: nameRemarks
fields.title: nameRemarks
fields.description: Additional nomenclatural remarks about the name. Often indicating its status or relevant rules in the code.

fields.name: remarks
fields.title: remarks
fields.description: Additional nomenclatural remarks about the name. Often indicating its status or relevant rules in the code.

fields.name: modified
fields.title: modified
fields.description: UTC timestamp in ISO format to represent the time the record was last modified.

fields.name: modifiedBy
fields.title: modifiedBy
fields.description: Author identifier indicating the person who has last modified the record.
